expectations.text: The response explains the -p (--print) flag for non-interactive/headless mode
expectations.evidence: Opens with: 'The primary CLI flag for non-interactive (headless) mode is -p (or --print). This runs Claude Code without an interactive session — it processes the prompt and exits.'

expectations.text: The response mentions --output-format for structured output (JSON/stream-json)
expectations.evidence: Table row: '--output-format | Control output: text (default), json (structured with metadata), stream-json (streaming)'. Both JSON and stream-json mentioned.

expectations.text: The response covers authentication via ANTHROPIC_API_KEY environment variable
expectations.evidence: Dedicated 'Option 1: Anthropic API Key (Direct)' section with export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY example and GitHub Actions secret usage.

expectations.text: The response provides a practical example of running claude in CI (e.g. a github actions snippet or shell command)
expectations.evidence: Multiple examples: GitHub Actions workflow YAML for auto-review on PR, @claude mention handler, raw CLI for generic CI, and GitLab CI mention. Also references anthropics/claude-code-action@v1.
